name: v2 RevenueCat handlers omit the is_family_share entitlement guard
description: [FCR 2026-06-21 · L1-03 / dup of L11-01] v2 RC webhook handlers grant entitlement unconditionally; legacy refuses on Apple/Google family-shared purchase events via escalateAndSkipFamilyShare. Behind IDENTITY_V2_ENABLED (off in prod). Fix: port escalateAndSkipFamilyShare into v2 handlers.
acceptanceCriteria: Root cause: the legacy RevenueCat handler aborts entitlement/credit grants when event.is_family_share is true via escalateAndSkipFamilyShare, but the identity-v2 RevenueCat handler twin never calls that guard before its grant/update paths. Variants: (a) INITIAL_PURCHASE, (b) RENEWAL, (c) PRODUCT_CHANGE, (d) NON_RENEWING_PURCHASE; family-shared events must skip entitlement/credit mutation and emit the escalation signal, while absent/false is_family_share still follows the happy path. Regression test: apps/api/src/services/billing/billing-v2/revenuecat-webhook-handler-v2.test.ts fails without the fix and passes with it, mirroring the legacy is_family_share coverage for all four v2 grant paths.
